cards.back: The process of finding the hidden state at each time step in a Recurrent Neural Network
A type of neural network where connections between time steps are made to capture temporal relationships in data
An RNN that processes input sequences in both forward and backward directions, allowing it to capture both past and future dependencies
A type of RNN with multiple hidden layers, capable of learning long-range dependencies in data
A class of neural networks that can be viewed as RNNs, often used for tasks such as graph processing and natural language processing
